input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on macOS:

---

## 1. Install Prerequisites

| Tool | Source | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| **Xcode** | Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/) | iOS SDK, simulators, build tools |
| **Xcode Command Line Tools** | `xcode-select --install` | CLI compilers & headers |
| **.NET SDK (latest LTS or STS)** | [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) | Runtime + CLI + MAUI workload |
| **JDK 17** | Installed automatically with the MAUI workload (or [Microsoft OpenJDK](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download)) | Android build tooling |
| **Android SDK** | Installed automatically with the MAUI workload | Android platform targets |

> **Optional IDE:** [Visual Studio Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/) with the **.NET MAUI extension**, or **JetBrains Rider**.

---

## 2. Step-by-Step Commands

```bash
# 1. Accept the Xcode license (after installing Xcode)
sudo xcodebuild -license accept

# 2. Install the .NET MAUI workload
dotnet workload install maui

# 3. Discover what you have installed
dotnet --list-sdks
dotnet workload list
```

The `maui` workload pulls in `android`, `ios`, `maccatalyst`, and the Android SDK + OpenJDK automatically.

---

## 3. Discover the Right Versions

| What | How to Check |
|------|-------------|
| Latest .NET SDK | `dotnet --list-sdks` or [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) |
| Supported Xcode version | [Apple release notes](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/xcode-release-notes) and the [MAUI release notes](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/whats-new/) |
| Available workloads | `dotnet workload search` |
| Installed Android SDKs | `dotnet android sdk list` or check `~/Library/Android/sdk` |

**Key compatibility rule:** each .NET MAUI release specifies a minimum Xcode version. Check the MAUI release notes before upgrading either one.

---

## 4. Verify Everything Works

```bash
# Create a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for all targets (quick sanity check)
dotnet build

# Run on iOS simulator
dotnet build -t:Run -f net9.0-ios

# Run on Android emulator (start one first via Android SDK)
dotnet build -t:Run -f net9.0-android
```

For the Android emulator, create an AVD:
```bash
# List available system images & create a device
$HOME/Library/Android/sdk/cmdline-tools/latest/bin/avdmanager \
  list device
$HOME/Library/Android/sdk/emulator/emulator -list-avds
```

---

## 5. Troubleshooting Checklist

- **`dotnet workload` fails** → update the SDK: `dotnet workload update`
- **iOS build fails** → verify `xcode-select -p` points to your Xcode app
- **Android build fails** → ensure `ANDROID_HOME` is set (`~/Library/Android/sdk`)
- **Simulator not found** → open Xcode → Settings → Platforms → download a simulator runtime

All sources above are official Microsoft/Apple channels. Adjust `net9.0` to whatever SDK version you install (e.g., `net8.0` for LTS).
